reason: These test different types (string, int64, int32, uint64) with different expected values - intentionally distinct
These are table-driven test cases with different types that intentionally test different behavior (int64, int32, uint64)

These three tests verify MarshalText/UnmarshalText for three semantically distinct enum types (Priority, Status, Trigger). While the test pattern is structurally identical, each test explicitly validates a specific enum type, maintaining clarity about which type is being tested. Refactoring into a generic helper would reduce readability without meaningful maintenance benefit, as enum types in Go are tested explicitly rather than generically.

The `testErrorChecker` generic helper function (lines 9-24) already extracts the common pattern. Each test function merely calls this helper with different parameters specific to the error type being tested. The "duplicates" are just the unique test case slices for different error types — inherently different data, not repeated logic.
